items.title: Train/validation/test splits
items.relation: defines which partitions metrics are computed on
items.description: Train, validation, and test splits determine where each logged scalar comes from— this topic names and records metrics; Train/Val/Test Splits defines the disjoint partitions themselves.

items.title: Precision, recall, and F1
items.description: When classification experiments log val/f1 or test/f1, the metric definitions and interpretation live in Precision/Recall/F1— tracking ensures those values are recorded with consistent split prefixes.

items.title: RMSE and MAE
items.description: Regression runs log val/rmse and val/mae using the definitions from RMSE and MAE— this topic covers naming and when to log them, not the error formulas.

items.title: MLflow and W&B
items.description: Experiment trackers store the train/val/test scalars and learning curves this topic defines— MLflow & W&B is the canonical home for tool APIs, run IDs, and dashboard comparison.

items.title: Loss functions
items.relation: produces the train loss scalar that
items.description: train/loss is the optimization objective from Loss Functions— it is logged every step; validation loss uses the same function on held-out data and may diverge from task metrics like F1.

items.title: Early stopping
items.description: Early stopping monitors a validation scalar (e.g. val/loss) each epoch and halts when it stops improving— the curve quality and naming conventions here determine whether stopping triggers correctly.
